response.justification: Aisha is a talented senior engineer with valuable geospatial expertise and strong software craftsmanship practices, but the technical fit is misaligned. Her C++ experience is 11+ years old (2014-2015), and her decade of subsequent work has been entirely in web/application stacks (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang). No Rust experience is evident. While the job explicitly welcomes learning new skills, jumping from web development to leading critical routing infrastructure in an unfamiliar language represents excessive technical risk for a role requiring immediate productivity.
strengths: Deep geospatial data expertise as GISCollective founder and from multiple GIS projects
Proven ability to build and operate distributed systems at scale (Dunnhumby: millions of requests/second)
Senior-level engineering maturity with strong TDD, quality assurance, and software craftsmanship practice
concerns: C++ skills are stale (last used 2014-2015); no Rust experience—the primary languages required for this role
Career trajectory shows 10+ year progression toward web/application development away from systems programming
Navigation/routing systems experience is absent; GIS background is mapping-focused, not routing-optimization focused
